title: What NOT to Do
subtitle: Use computers safely and carefully!
overview: Students learn safe and responsible computer usage habits through fun role-play activities. They understand what actions can damage computers and how to handle devices carefully in real life.

images._note: Add actual classroom photos and role-play activities from the session.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students typing gently on keyboard
gallery.caption: Use keyboard softly and carefully

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Teacher showing safe cable handling
gallery.caption: Do not pull wires forcefully

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students sitting properly in computer lab
gallery.caption: Proper computer lab behavior

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students acting good and bad computer habits
gallery.caption: Fun role-play activity during session

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Washing hands before computer use
gallery.caption: Keep hands clean before touching devices

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Teacher explaining no food near computer
gallery.caption: Food and water should stay away from electronics

keyTopics: Never pull or unplug wires forcefully
Keep food and water away from computers
Use mouse gently — no throwing or hitting
Always ask teacher before changing connections
Keep hands clean before touching devices
Switch OFF devices safely after use

activity.title: Good Habit vs Bad Habit Role Play
parts: Flash cards showing good and bad habits
steps: Teacher demonstrates a bad habit like banging keys
Discuss why safety rules are important

quiz.q: What should you do while typing on a keyboard?
quiz.explanation: Keyboards should be used gently to avoid damage.

quiz.q: Should you pull computer wires forcefully?
quiz.explanation: Pulling wires can damage computers and may be dangerous.

quiz.q: What should stay away from computers?
quiz.explanation: Water and food can damage electronic devices.

quiz.q: How should we use a mouse?
quiz.explanation: Mouse should be handled softly and carefully.

quiz.q: Who should you ask before changing computer connections?
quiz.explanation: Always ask a teacher or adult before changing wires or settings.

quiz.q: Why should hands be clean before using computers?
options: To keep devices clean and safe
quiz.explanation: Clean hands help protect devices from dirt and damage.

quiz.q: What is the correct way to sit while using a computer?
quiz.explanation: Good posture helps safe and comfortable computer use.

quiz.q: What should you do after using a computer?
quiz.explanation: Devices should always be shut down safely.

quiz.q: Why are computer safety rules important?
options: To keep devices safe and working properly
quiz.explanation: Safety rules protect both students and devices.
